channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
item.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump is weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Philanthropy is a 'significant form of power.' Here's how Jeffrey Epstein exploited that
item.description: A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.
item.content:encoded:
A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.

(Image credit: Hanna Barczyk for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Trump administration cuts turned rural towns into sitting ducks for disasters
item.description: The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.

(Image credit: Mel Evans)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: How a SCOTUS decision on birthright citizenship could impact education access
item.description: All children, regardless of immigration status, have the right to a free K-12 public education. But without birthright citizenship, access to schools and colleges could get complicated.
item.content:encoded: All children, regardless of immigration status, have the right to a free K-12 public education. But without birthright citizenship, access to schools and colleges could get complicated.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: China's chatbot industry is fiercely competing for customers. Cue the freebies
item.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

(Image credit: ADEK BERRY/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: As birthright citizenship goes to Supreme Court, here's how Americans feel about it
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments Wednesday on whether all children born in the United States can continue to automatically receive citizenship.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court will hear arguments Wednesday on whether all children born in the United States can continue to automatically receive citizenship.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: How much protein do you need? Here's how to personalize your optimal intake
item.description: The Dietary Guidelines released this year recommend higher levels of this essential nutrient. But protein needs are personal. Here's how to assess yours.
item.content:encoded: The Dietary Guidelines released this year recommend higher levels of this essential nutrient. But protein needs are personal. Here's how to assess yours.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Over one million Lebanese displaced by Israel's invasion, thousands of U.S. troops deployed to the Middle East, with more on the way, delays continue at U.S. airports with no funding deal in sight.
item.content:encoded: Over one million Lebanese displaced by Israel's invasion, thousands of U.S. troops deployed to the Middle East, with more on the way, delays continue at U.S. airports with no funding deal in sight.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
